purpose: Canonical eligibility, ownership, entitlement-window, and selection-mode contract for the shared MMG digital-asset catalog.

system_boundaries.knowledge_library: One customer-facing surface with Public Catalog, Subscription Selection, and My Library modes backed by the same canonical asset identity.

rules: One asset_id represents one commercial digital asset across Shopify, Kairos, the Knowledge Library, the Customer Portal, and delivery records.
A Shopify product handle may change only when redirects and registry mappings are updated; asset_id remains stable.
Ownership, delivery, and recommendation systems must join on asset_id rather than product title.

status_semantics.draft: Incomplete and excluded from every public or subscriber catalog.
status_semantics.approved: Content approved but not yet released for active storefront or subscription delivery.
status_semantics.active: Approved, published, customer-ready, and eligible for the modes allowed by the remaining metadata.
status_semantics.retired: No longer selectable or purchasable; existing owners retain access unless an entitlement is explicitly revoked.

authoritative_filter: Shopify product is published to the Online Store channel.
public_catalog.subscription_indicator_rule: Show Included with Membership only when the complete subscription-selection metadata predicate also passes before customer-specific ownership and window checks.

subscription_selection.access: authenticated customer with an active MMG Knowledge Subscription contract
authoritative_filter: mmg.subscription_value is an integer greater than or equal to 1.
Customer does not already own the asset.
The current entitlement window is open.
The asset subscription value does not exceed the customer's remaining unreserved units.
The asset is not already selected in the current entitlement window.
subscription_selection.client_rule: Liquid and browser JavaScript may display a provisional eligibility hint, but Kairos must revalidate authentication, subscription status, ownership, entitlement capacity, delivery readiness, and window state before accepting a selection.

my_library.retention_rule: Retired assets remain visible to existing owners unless the entitlement has been explicitly revoked.

ownership_contract.owned_definition: A customer owns an asset when at least one non-revoked active grant exists for the canonical asset_id.
ownership_contract.deduplication_rule: Multiple qualifying grants may be preserved for audit history, but the customer-facing library displays one asset entry per asset_id.
ownership_contract.selection_rule: An owned asset is never offered as a new subscription selection and consumes no additional entitlement units.

unit_accounting.total_units: Units assigned to the current package window.
unit_accounting.selected_units: Units attached to confirmed or currently selected assets.

scheduled_package_review.close_behavior: Confirmed assets receive active subscription-delivery grants and enter My Library.

entitlement_window_contract.transaction_rule: Selection confirmation must revalidate every asset and remaining units before creating delivery grants.

storefront_integration.data_role: Expose public product metadata and a provisional catalog eligibility hint for the future picker without exposing private ownership or entitlement decisions.
security_rules: Do not serialize customer ownership grants into public page source.
Do not serialize subscription contract identifiers into public page source.
Do not accept a browser-calculated remaining-unit total as authoritative.
Do not grant an asset from Liquid or storefront JavaScript.

asset_registry.release_rule: A registry record may be publicly discoverable while remaining blocked from subscription selection until all required media, delivery, Shopify metafield, and runtime identifier gates pass.

integration_sequence.current_component: MMG Knowledge Library eligibility metadata and selection-mode contract

release_gates: Shopify metafield definitions exist and are pinned for product editors where appropriate.
Every selectable product has a unique stable asset_id.
Every selectable product passes the complete metadata and delivery-package predicate.
Kairos resolves active subscription status and ownership server-side.
Entitlement-window unit accounting rejects over-selection and duplicate confirmation.
The Knowledge Library does not expose services or the subscription product as selectable digital assets.
The customer cannot select an already-owned asset.
First-package selection is exactly two units and occurs after checkout.
Future package review windows remain between 24 and 48 hours.
Public catalog, subscription selection, and My Library mode boundaries pass end-to-end authorization QA.
